item.title: Qatari gas shipment clears Strait of Hormuz after Pakistan-Iran talks
item.description: Tanker carrying LNG from Qatar throws energy lifeline to Islamabad

item.title: How top Wall Street law firms became a pipeline for insider trading
item.description: Deal advice has evolved into a huge legal business but leaves firms vulnerable to misuse of confidential information

item.title: ‘It’s shameful’: New York’s elite lash out at Mamdani’s second-home tax
item.description: The city’s wealthy property owners say they are sick of being vilified

item.title: Tank maker KNDS pushes Berlin to decide on taking stake before IPO
item.description: German family shareholders want to press ahead with listing of Paris-backed group at €15bn-€20bn valuation

item.title: Arsenal fans face 10-fold rise in Champions League final flight and hotel costs
item.description: Premier League club to face Paris Saint-Germain on May 30 in Budapest

item.title: Saudi Aramco reports higher profits despite Iran war
item.description: East-west pipeline allows oil company to circumvent the Strait of Hormuz and keep pumping

item.title: OpenAI trial lays bare rivalries behind start-up’s $852bn rise
item.description: Lawsuit brought by Elon Musk heads into final week in court, with Sam Altman due to testify

item.title: Africa’s richest man eyes Kenya for new refinery
item.description: Aliko Dangote tells the FT he is leaning towards Mombasa rather than Tanzania for 650,000-barrel-a-day oil mega-project

item.title: European carmakers take €8bn hit from Trump tariffs
item.description: US president has threatened to raise levies to 25% if EU does not implement last year’s trade deal

item.title: Goldman-backed Lendable plots US expansion after outpacing banks on loans
item.description: Consumer credit fintech issued more new personal loans than any other UK lender

item.title: Germany in fresh push to buy Tomahawks after Trump row
item.description: Defence minister plans Washington trip after Chancellor Friedrich Merz falls out with US president

item.title: Buckle up: plane tickets could still get much more expensive
item.description: Airlines have managed to avoid greater turbulence because jet fuel hasn’t actually run short — yet

item.title: Australia’s ‘petro-diplomacy’ eases fuel shortage fears
item.description: Canberra has used its status as major gas exporter to push Asian suppliers to maintain flow of vital refined oil products

item.title: ‘I’m not Mary Poppins’: Mike Ashley admits to orchestrating downfall of Peter Cowgill
item.description: In a rare interview the Sports Direct founder confirms he was behind the car park video that led to his rival’s demise

item.title: The ‘surname ceiling’ holding back family companies
item.description: Many of the brightest workers steer clear of such businesses because promotions are scarcer and rewards lower

item.title: Chinese bubble tea chain with more stores than McDonald’s wants to conquer the world
item.description: Mixue has bold plans for international expansion but its successful formula may face hurdles abroad

item.title: Iran war boosts Panama Canal’s revenues by up to 15%
item.description: CFO Victor Vial says world trading routes will be permanently reshaped after the conflict

item.title: America’s right and left have perfected each other’s tricks
item.description: Both extremes now have an affinity for cancel culture and conspiracy theories

item.title: Retail investors cool on private equity as jitters spread
item.description: Fundraising by evergreen vehicles has slowed amid worries about exposure to software and AI

item.title: Women at the sharp end as AI takes over administrative roles
item.description: Female-dominated clerical work is among the most vulnerable to automation, and labour market losses are already being felt

item.title: Tenerife prepares to evacuate hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn

item.title: Drone start-up Helsing set for $18bn valuation as investors pile into defence
item.description: German company backed by Spotify’s Daniel Ek set to raise $1.2bn in latest funding round

item.title: Iowa pension chief resigns amid concerns about performance figures
item.description: Former risk officer alleges executives used misleading benchmarks and risk measures to improve results

item.title: New York Stock Exchange to open private members’ club on Wall Street
item.description: Exclusive social hub planned for renovated vault as NYSE competes with Nasdaq for lucrative tech IPOs

item.title: NBA Europe’s path to profit
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: what a boutique bank takeover says about sports

item.title: WHSmith’s new owner makes a contrarian bet on the UK high street
item.description: Modella Capital plans to cut as many as 150 WHSmith stores out of the 480 it acquired and bring in new goods and services

item.title: Standard Life and CVC square the circle on defined-benefit pensions
item.description: There are more than £1tn of assets sitting in UK retirement schemes that could be picked up in risk transfer deals

item.title: Europe’s €14tn cash pile benefits banks not retail investors, BlackRock warns
item.description: World’s largest asset manager says governments must address under-investment of retail money in capital markets

item.title: Retail investors turn away from commercial property holdings
item.description: Rising interest rates and lower demand have hit returns in the past decade

item.title: The sailors who keep ‘ghost’ tankers moving
item.description: Working on an oil tanker under sanctions was a job like any other — until they ended up in the line of fire
